content.text: Timeline:
2021 – Anthropic founded
Ex‑OpenAI leaders including Dario and Daniela Amodei found Anthropic in San Francisco as a Public Benefit Corporation focused on safe, interpretable AI.
Raises around 124M USD seed/Series A funding from early investors like Jaan Tallinn, Dustin Moskovitz, and others.
2022 – Early research and major funding
Anthropic develops early Claude prototypes and Constitutional AI ideas while focusing on AI safety research.
Secures a large Series B (hundreds of millions of dollars) to accelerate Claude development.
2023 – First public Claude release
March 2023: Claude 1 launches publicly, introducing Constitutional AI alignment and large context windows.
July 2023: Claude 2 and the API become widely available, improving reasoning and coding.
Amazon and Google announce multibillion‑dollar investment agreements in Anthropic (up to 4B from Amazon and around 2B from Google).
2024 – Claude 3 family and rapid growth
March 2024: Anthropic releases the Claude 3 family (Haiku, Sonnet, Opus), with strong scores on benchmarks like MMLU and GPQA.
June 2024: Claude 3.5 Sonnet launches with faster performance and better coding, becoming a popular coding assistant.
2025 – Next‑gen Claude and enterprise push
2025: Claude 4 series launches with enhanced reasoning, larger context, and stronger software engineering capabilities.
Anthropic’s valuation climbs well into the hundreds of billions in some reports as it becomes a leading enterprise AI provider.
2026 – Latest releases and global role
Early–mid 2026: Anthropic continues releasing updated Claude 4.x and “Fable 5 / Mythos‑class” models, pushing frontier‑model capabilities and long‑context reasoning.
The company becomes a central voice in global AI safety and governance debates, updating its Responsible Scaling Policy and working with governments on frontier AI standards.
